text: The surgeon begins the procedure by mobilizing the left colon, preserving the superior hypogastric plexus. The avascular layer between the mesocolon and retroperitoneum is separated, followed by high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery and vein. The left mesocolon is further mobilized, with hypogastric nerves identified and preserved behind the rectosigmoid. The prehypogastric nerve fascia is incised to enter the presacral space, where sharp dissection is used to dissect the vascular presacral space. The lateral attachment of the left colon is dissected, fully mobilizing the colon up to the splenic flexure.

The surgeon performs a nerve-preserving pelvic dissection, carefully preserving the whitish connective tissue on the autonomic nerves. Sharp dissection is maintained between the yellowish mesocolon and the whitish connective tissue, with good counter traction aiding the process.

The surgeon dissects the end-pelvic fascia, exposing the surface of the levator muscle. On the anterior side, the whitish connective tissue on the seminal vesicle is preserved. Behind the seminal vesicle, the neurovascular bundle is identified and preserved with its covering connective tissue. As the dissection progresses distally, the neurovascular bundle is seen approaching medially.

The surgeon exposes the prostate and begins intersphincteric dissection between the pubococcygeal muscle and rectal wall. The rectococcygeal muscle is divided posterior to the rectal wall, with careful attention to avoid injury. The dissection proceeds inside the puborectal muscle, separating the intersphincteric plane. The neurovascular bundle is preserved, and the recto-urethral muscle is visible, attaching anteriorly behind the distal prostate.

The surgeon approaches the anus, closing the rectal lumen with purse string sutures. The mucosa and internal sphincter muscle are incised above the dentate line, accessing the intersphincteric plane. Anteriorly, the pink recto-irrational muscle and whitish perineal body are visible. The specimen is retrieved from the anus, and anastomosis is completed using sixteen vertical mattress sutures.
